title: The Do-While Loop 🎬
description: A loop that guarantees it will run at least one time before checking conditions.

slides.title: Act First, Ask Later 🎭
slides.content:
# The Guaranteed Run

A regular `while` loop checks the condition FIRST. If it's false immediately, the loop never runs at all.

But what if you HAVE to do something exactly once before checking?
For example: "Ask the user for their password, THEN check if it's correct."

slides.title: Syntax 📝

quizData.question: What is the key difference between a `while` loop and a `do-while` loop?

options.text: A `do-while` loop is guaranteed to run its code block at least one time.
A `do-while` loop is used for counting numbers.

quizData.explanation: Because `do-while` checks the condition at the END of the loop, the code inside the block will always execute at least once.
The 'do' keyword initiates the block before the 'while' condition is written.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Run this! Even though `lives == 0` from the start, it will STILL print 'Game Over' once!
